explanation: This command disables audit logging for logon events, which matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria by using the auditpol.exe binary with potentially evasive flags.
This command removes all audit policies, which could be used to bypass detection by clearing audit settings. It aligns with the Sigma rule's focus on audit policy tampering.

This command disables command-line logging in the registry, which indirectly supports evasion by reducing visibility of malicious command-line activity. It does not directly match the Sigma rule but supports the broader evasion objective.
